simulation_name: Panguard ATR Attack Prediction - AI Agent Security 2026-2027
description: Simulate the AI agent security ecosystem to predict novel attack techniques and generate pre-emptive ATR detection rules.

world_setup.environment: A digital twin of the AI agent security ecosystem. Thousands of AI agents (LLM tools, MCP skills, autonomous agents) operate across enterprises, startups, and open-source projects. Attackers constantly probe for new vulnerabilities while defenders evolve detection rules.
world_setup.initial_state: The Panguard ATR rule set covers 32 known attack patterns across 9 categories. Behavioral fingerprinting has just been deployed. The community consensus system is active with ~500 participating nodes.

god_view_injections.event: A major MCP skill registry is compromised, allowing attackers to push updates to popular skills
A new multimodal LLM is released with vision capabilities, opening new attack surfaces for image-based injection
An enterprise deploys 1000 autonomous agents with inter-agent communication, creating a large multi-agent attack surface
Behavioral fingerprinting evasion techniques are published in a security research paper, giving attackers new ideas
A coordinated Sybil attack targets the community consensus system with 50 fake nodes

expected_deliverables: 10-30 new ATR rule predictions per simulation run
Coverage gap analysis for existing 32 rules
Recommended detection patterns for each predicted attack
